description: Live-LLM A/B comparison driving each transcript through BOTH the unified-tutor grade prompt AND the split gradeAgent prompt. Consumed by packages/agent/evals/grader-ab.ts behind LEARNPRO_RUN_LIVE_LLM_EVAL=1. NOT consumed by the canned-prompt-eval pipeline.

transcripts.label: py-two-sum-hash-map-clean
transcripts.problem_statement: Return the indices of the two numbers in `nums` that sum to `target`. Each input has exactly one solution; do not use the same element twice.
transcripts.notes: Idiomatic single-pass dict lookup. Expect both rubrics to score idiomatic high.

transcripts.label: py-two-sum-nested-loop-brute
transcripts.problem_statement: Return the indices of the two numbers in `nums` that sum to `target`. Each input has exactly one solution; do not use the same element twice.
transcripts.notes: Brute-force O(n^2) passes tests. Headline split-vs-unified test: split grader should drop idiomatic + efficiency, unified rubric tends to anchor near 0.7-0.9.

transcripts.label: py-filter-evens-list-comp
transcripts.problem_statement: Return a new list containing only the even integers from the input, preserving order.
transcripts.user_code:
def solve(nums):
    return [n for n in nums if n % 2 == 0]
transcripts.notes: Clean Python comprehension; expect high idiomatic on both rubrics.

transcripts.label: py-filter-evens-imperative-loop
transcripts.problem_statement: Return a new list containing only the even integers from the input, preserving order.
transcripts.notes: Imperative append-loop where a comprehension is idiomatic. Split grader should score idiomatic lower than unified would.

transcripts.label: py-range-sum-off-by-one-fail
transcripts.problem_statement: Given two integers `start` and `end`, return the sum of every integer from `start` to `end` inclusive.
failing_test_summaries: mismatch; expected=15 got=10 (start=1, end=5 should include 5)
mismatch; expected=10 got=6 (start=1, end=4 should include 4)
transcripts.notes: Off-by-one in range bounds. Expect test_coverage_thinking=1 on split; correctness 0.6 on unified.

transcripts.label: ts-camelcase-deep-clean
transcripts.problem_statement: Given an object with snake_case keys (including nested objects and arrays), return a new object where every key is camelCased recursively.
transcripts.notes: Clean idiomatic TS with recursion + narrowing. Expect both rubrics to score idiomatic high.

transcripts.label: ts-equality-coercion-bug
transcripts.problem_statement: Given an array of strings that may contain numeric strings, return the sum of items strictly equal to the integer 42.
failing_test_summaries: mismatch; expected=0 got=2 (["42", "42"] should sum to 0 under strict equality)
mismatch; expected=0 got=1 (["42"] should sum to 0 under strict equality)
transcripts.notes: Loose equality bug; idiomatic TS uses === plus explicit Number(). Expect split idiomatic=1 / test_coverage_thinking=1.

transcripts.label: py-memoized-fib-clean
transcripts.problem_statement: Return the nth Fibonacci number. n can be as large as 10000.
transcripts.notes: lru_cache memoization is the canonical Python idiom for this problem. Expect both rubrics to score idiomatic high; split efficiency=5.
